# Liquid Glass

> Beautiful, accessible Liquid Glass components for React with real SVG/CSS
> refraction and production-ready fallbacks.

`@clean99/liquid-glass` is a React UI component library for Apple-inspired
Liquid Glass interfaces on the web. It owns the component API, accessibility
contract, design tokens, fallback strategy, shadcn-style Registry metadata,
Storybook documentation, and release gates. Enhanced refraction is delegated to
`@hashintel/refractive`.

The package is prepared for public release but is not published to npm yet.
Do not describe npm install commands, shadcn registry install commands,
GitHub Pages deployment, or exact Kube parity as live until the matching public
run has succeeded.

## Primary Docs

- [README](README.md): repository first screen, current status, quick start,
  quality gates, and release status.
- [Documentation index](docs/index.md): public documentation map and component
  navigation.
- [Installation](docs/installation.md): install path, peer dependencies, CSS
  imports, and Next.js usage after npm publication.
- [Adoption guide](docs/adoption-guide.md): current adoption status, good fits,
  poor fits, integration paths, and launch blockers.
- [API overview](docs/api-overview.md): provider, modes, component API groups,
  and export shape.
- [Accessibility](docs/accessibility.md): native-first semantics, ARIA patterns,
  keyboard and focus behavior, a11y gates, and known limits.
- [Browser support](docs/browser-support.md): enhanced, fallback, solid, and
  unsupported browser behavior.

## Components And Registry

- [Component inventory](docs/component-inventory.md): implemented and planned
  component inventory.
- [Component map](docs/components/map.md): shadcn-style directory for every
  implemented public component, including source, Storybook, visual profile,
  registry, and written page status.
- [Component documentation contract](docs/component-documentation.md):
  per-component page standard for status, install honesty, usage, anatomy, API,
  visual states, accessibility, registry entries, and verification evidence.
- [Component docs](docs/components/index.md): package-backed pages for
  Provider, Surface, Button, Card, Field, and Dialog.
- [Component inventory data](docs/component-inventory.json): machine-readable
  component status.
- [shadcn-style Registry](docs/shadcn-registry.md): registry files and
  post-npm-publish install path.
- [Registry root](registry.json): shadcn-compatible registry metadata.
- [Flat registry item](liquid-glass.json): package-backed registry entry.
- [Package registry item](registry/liquid-glass.json): registry copy that must
  stay equivalent to the flat item.

## Visual Docs And Testing

- [Visual documentation](docs/visual-documentation.md): Storybook Pages,
  component states, screenshots, accessibility, and Kube reference evidence.
- [Visual state coverage](docs/visual-state-coverage.json): machine-readable
  visual evidence map.
- [Testing strategy](docs/testing.md): local gates, CI gates, Storybook,
  accessibility, package, and Kube reference checks.
- [Kube parity gate](docs/kube-parity-gate.md): strict and exact reference gate
  semantics.

## Governance And Release

- [Open-source governance](docs/open-source-governance.md): benchmark against
  shadcn/ui, Radix UI, Chakra UI, and HeroUI.
- [Maintainer runbook](docs/maintainer-runbook.md): maintainer triage, CI
  failure handling, Pages, release, security, registry, and rollback procedures.
- [Governance scorecard](docs/governance-scorecard.md): local and remote-aware
  readiness audit commands.
- [Repository settings](docs/github-repository-settings.md): GitHub Pages,
  branch protection, secrets, topics, and maintainer commands.
- [Release checklist](docs/open-source-release.md): npm, provenance, Pages,
  rollback, and release readiness checklist.
- [Roadmap](ROADMAP.md): staged public launch plan.
- [Contributing](CONTRIBUTING.md): contributor workflow and required checks.
- [Security policy](SECURITY.md): vulnerability reporting and supported
  versions.
- [Support](SUPPORT.md): issue routing and support boundaries.
- [Attributions](ATTRIBUTIONS.md): third-party references, licenses, and
  no-copied-source policy.

## Assistant Rules

- Use Node `>=22.13.0`, pnpm `>=11`, and GitHub Actions Node 24.
- Run `pnpm format`, `pnpm lint`, `pnpm typecheck`, `pnpm test:docs`,
  `pnpm test:release-readiness`, and `pnpm test:unit` after changes.
- Run `pnpm test:registry` when registry or package metadata changes.
- Do not copy third-party source. Add new references to `ATTRIBUTIONS.md` or
  `docs/reference-provenance.json`.
- Do not claim npm publication, GitHub Pages deployment, or exact Kube parity
  without a successful public run.
- Treat `pnpm test:kube-reference:strict` as the release-candidate visual gate.
  `pnpm test:kube-reference:exact` is the separate final exact-parity target.

## Optional Deep Context

- [Optics architecture](docs/optics-architecture.md): refraction, edge masks,
  displacement maps, foreground handling, and physics tests.
- [Reference research](docs/reference-research.md): inspected public references
  and local implementation decisions.
- [Reference provenance](docs/reference-provenance.json): machine-readable
  provenance and no-copied-source evidence.
- [UI library benchmark](docs/ui-library-benchmark.md): quantified comparison
  with mainstream React UI libraries.
